---
title: "Jeongho Choi"
image: jeongho.jpg
order: 1
about:
  template: solana
  image: jeongho.jpg
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/Jeongho-Choi
    - icon: globe
      text: website
      href: https://jeongho-choi.github.io
    - icon: envelope
      text: email
      href: mailto:jeonghochoi@khu.ac.kr
    - icon: chat-left-quote
      text: "Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=JShplMsAAAAJ&hl=en
    
---

Jeongho is an assistant professor in the deparment of Political Science at Kyung Hee University. His research focuses on comparative politics, public opinion, and political behavior, particularly exploring populism, political representation, public support for democracy, and democratic backsliding. Methodologically, he uses various quantitative methods, including multilevel modeling, computational text analysis, and machine learning. His dissertation investigates how issue-congruence between the public and political parties influences public support for populism in different regions and argues that public support for populism is a response to discontent with contemporary democratic systems.
